description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a predominantly white and light gray surface, interspersed with patches of brown and darker gray. The rock's surface is rough and uneven, featuring natural crevices, bumps, and subtle layering that suggest weathering and exposure to the elements. Some areas display a chalky or powdery appearance, while others are more solid and compact, hinting at a mix of mineral compositions. The coloration is mottled, with lighter tones dominating the upper surfaces and darker, earthier hues concentrated on the lower and side regions. The rock appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or openings, and its form is asymmetrical, making it look natural and unmodified. Its size and shape suggest it could serve as a natural barrier or decorative element in an urban park or landscaped area.
description: A rough, irregularly shaped white and gray rock with mottled brown patches and a weathered, natural surface. Its dense, solid form and asymmetrical contours make it suitable as a barrier or landscape feature.
description_view_0: This view highlights the rock's broad, white upper surface with subtle creases and a few brownish streaks along the side.
description_view_1: The rock appears rounder and darker from this angle, with more pronounced brown and gray mottling and a rougher texture.
description_view_2: This side shows a mix of white and dark gray, with a more compact, slightly pointed end and visible surface irregularities.
description_view_3: The rock's lighter, chalky surface is most prominent here, with gentle curves and less visible dark patches compared to other views.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped sidewalk edge
